concepts.label: Number
concepts.definition: A mathematical object used to count, measure, and label

concepts.label: Natural Number
concepts.definition: The positive integers used for counting: 1, 2, 3, ...

concepts.label: Integer
concepts.definition: Whole numbers including positive, negative, and zero

concepts.label: Rational Number
concepts.definition: Numbers that can be expressed as fractions p/q where p and q are integers

concepts.label: Irrational Number
concepts.definition: Numbers that cannot be expressed as simple fractions

concepts.label: Real Number
concepts.definition: All rational and irrational numbers combined

concepts.label: Complex Number
concepts.definition: Numbers of the form a + bi where i is the imaginary unit

concepts.label: Prime Number
concepts.definition: A natural number greater than 1 with exactly two divisors: 1 and itself

concepts.label: Composite Number
concepts.definition: A natural number with more than two divisors

concepts.label: Variable
concepts.definition: A symbol representing an unknown or changing quantity

concepts.label: Constant
concepts.definition: A fixed value that does not change

concepts.label: Expression
concepts.definition: A mathematical phrase combining numbers, variables, and operations

concepts.label: Equation
concepts.definition: A statement that two expressions are equal

concepts.label: Inequality
concepts.definition: A statement comparing two expressions using <, >, ≤, or ≥

concepts.label: Function
concepts.definition: A relation that assigns exactly one output to each input

concepts.label: Domain
concepts.definition: The set of all possible input values for a function

concepts.label: Range
concepts.definition: The set of all possible output values for a function

concepts.label: Linear Function
concepts.definition: A function of the form f(x) = mx + b forming a straight line

concepts.label: Quadratic Function
concepts.definition: A function of the form f(x) = ax² + bx + c forming a parabola

concepts.label: Exponential Function
concepts.definition: A function of the form f(x) = a^x where a is a positive constant

concepts.label: Logarithmic Function
concepts.definition: The inverse of an exponential function

concepts.label: Polynomial
concepts.definition: An expression consisting of variables and coefficients using addition, subtraction, and multiplication

concepts.label: Degree
concepts.definition: The highest power of the variable in a polynomial

concepts.label: Coefficient
concepts.definition: The numerical factor of a term containing variables

concepts.label: Root
concepts.definition: A value that makes an equation equal to zero

concepts.label: Slope
concepts.definition: The rate of change of a line, calculated as rise over run

concepts.label: Y-intercept
concepts.definition: The point where a line crosses the y-axis

concepts.label: System of Equations
concepts.definition: A collection of equations with the same variables

concepts.label: Matrix
concepts.definition: A rectangular array of numbers arranged in rows and columns

concepts.label: Determinant
concepts.definition: A scalar value calculated from a square matrix

concepts.label: Vector
concepts.definition: A quantity having both magnitude and direction

concepts.label: Scalar
concepts.definition: A quantity having only magnitude, no direction

concepts.label: Dot Product
concepts.definition: A scalar product of two vectors

concepts.label: Cross Product
concepts.definition: A vector product of two vectors perpendicular to both

concepts.label: Limit
concepts.definition: The value that a function approaches as input approaches some value

concepts.label: Continuity
concepts.definition: A property where a function has no breaks, jumps, or holes

concepts.label: Derivative
concepts.definition: The rate of change of a function at a specific point

concepts.label: Integral
concepts.definition: The accumulation of quantities, the reverse process of differentiation

concepts.label: Definite Integral
concepts.definition: An integral with specific upper and lower bounds

concepts.label: Indefinite Integral
concepts.definition: An integral without specified bounds, representing a family of functions

concepts.label: Fundamental Theorem of Calculus
concepts.definition: The theorem connecting differentiation and integration

concepts.label: Chain Rule
concepts.definition: A formula for computing the derivative of composite functions

concepts.label: Product Rule
concepts.definition: A formula for finding the derivative of a product of functions

concepts.label: Quotient Rule
concepts.definition: A formula for finding the derivative of a quotient of functions

concepts.label: Critical Point
concepts.definition: A point where the derivative is zero or undefined

concepts.label: Maximum
concepts.definition: The largest value of a function in a given interval

concepts.label: Minimum
concepts.definition: The smallest value of a function in a given interval

concepts.label: Inflection Point
concepts.definition: A point where the concavity of a curve changes

concepts.label: Series
concepts.definition: The sum of terms in a sequence

concepts.label: Sequence
concepts.definition: An ordered list of numbers following a pattern

concepts.label: Convergence
concepts.definition: The property of a sequence or series approaching a finite limit

concepts.label: Divergence
concepts.definition: The property of a sequence or series not approaching a finite limit

concepts.label: Taylor Series
concepts.definition: An infinite series representation of a function using derivatives

concepts.label: Point
concepts.definition: A location in space with no dimensions

concepts.label: Line
concepts.definition: A straight path extending infinitely in both directions

concepts.label: Plane
concepts.definition: A flat surface extending infinitely in all directions

concepts.label: Angle
concepts.definition: The space between two intersecting lines measured in degrees or radians

concepts.label: Triangle
concepts.definition: A polygon with three sides and three angles

concepts.label: Square
concepts.definition: A quadrilateral with four equal sides and four right angles

concepts.label: Rectangle
concepts.definition: A quadrilateral with four right angles and opposite sides equal

concepts.label: Circle
concepts.definition: The set of all points equidistant from a center point

concepts.label: Radius
concepts.definition: The distance from the center of a circle to any point on the circle

concepts.label: Diameter
concepts.definition: A straight line passing through the center of a circle, twice the radius

concepts.label: Circumference
concepts.definition: The perimeter of a circle

concepts.label: Area
concepts.definition: The measure of the surface enclosed by a shape

concepts.label: Perimeter
concepts.definition: The distance around the boundary of a shape

concepts.label: Volume
concepts.definition: The amount of space occupied by a three-dimensional object

concepts.label: Surface Area
concepts.definition: The total area of all faces of a three-dimensional object

concepts.label: Cube
concepts.definition: A three-dimensional shape with six equal square faces

concepts.label: Sphere
concepts.definition: A three-dimensional shape where all points are equidistant from the center

concepts.label: Cylinder
concepts.definition: A three-dimensional shape with circular bases and straight sides

concepts.label: Cone
concepts.definition: A three-dimensional shape with a circular base tapering to a point

concepts.label: Pythagorean Theorem
concepts.definition: In a right triangle, a² + b² = c² where c is the hypotenuse

concepts.label: Trigonometry
concepts.definition: The study of relationships between angles and sides in triangles

concepts.label: Sine
concepts.definition: The ratio of opposite side to hypotenuse in a right triangle

concepts.label: Cosine
concepts.definition: The ratio of adjacent side to hypotenuse in a right triangle

concepts.label: Tangent
concepts.definition: The ratio of opposite side to adjacent side in a right triangle

concepts.label: Unit Circle
concepts.definition: A circle with radius 1 centered at the origin

concepts.label: Radian
concepts.definition: A unit of angular measurement where 2π radians = 360 degrees

concepts.label: Pi
concepts.definition: The ratio of a circle's circumference to its diameter, approximately 3.14159

concepts.label: Euler's Number
concepts.definition: The base of natural logarithms, approximately 2.71828

concepts.label: Set
concepts.definition: A collection of distinct objects considered as a whole

concepts.label: Element
concepts.definition: A member of a set

concepts.label: Subset
concepts.definition: A set whose elements are all contained in another set

concepts.label: Union
concepts.definition: The combination of all elements from two or more sets

concepts.label: Intersection
concepts.definition: The set of elements common to two or more sets

concepts.label: Empty Set
concepts.definition: A set containing no elements

concepts.label: Cardinality
concepts.definition: The number of elements in a set

concepts.label: Permutation
concepts.definition: An arrangement of objects where order matters

concepts.label: Combination
concepts.definition: A selection of objects where order doesn't matter

concepts.label: Factorial
concepts.definition: The product of all positive integers less than or equal to n

concepts.label: Probability
concepts.definition: A measure of the likelihood of an event occurring

concepts.label: Sample Space
concepts.definition: The set of all possible outcomes of an experiment

concepts.label: Event
concepts.definition: A subset of the sample space

concepts.label: Independent Events
concepts.definition: Events where the occurrence of one doesn't affect the other

concepts.label: Conditional Probability
concepts.definition: The probability of an event given that another event has occurred

concepts.label: Random Variable
concepts.definition: A function that assigns numerical values to outcomes of an experiment

concepts.label: Expected Value
concepts.definition: The average value of a random variable over many trials

concepts.label: Variance
concepts.definition: A measure of how spread out values are from the mean

concepts.label: Standard Deviation
concepts.definition: The square root of variance, measuring spread in original units

concepts.label: Normal Distribution
concepts.definition: A bell-shaped probability distribution symmetric about the mean

concepts.label: Mean
concepts.definition: The arithmetic average of a set of values

concepts.label: Median
concepts.definition: The middle value when data is arranged in order

concepts.label: Mode
concepts.definition: The most frequently occurring value in a dataset

concepts.label: Graph
concepts.definition: A collection of vertices connected by edges

concepts.label: Vertex
concepts.definition: A node or point in a graph

concepts.label: Edge
concepts.definition: A connection between two vertices in a graph

concepts.label: Tree
concepts.definition: A connected graph with no cycles

concepts.label: Path
concepts.definition: A sequence of vertices where each adjacent pair is connected by an edge

concepts.label: Cycle
concepts.definition: A path that starts and ends at the same vertex

concepts.label: Algorithm
concepts.definition: A step-by-step procedure for solving a problem

concepts.label: Proof
concepts.definition: A logical argument demonstrating the truth of a mathematical statement

concepts.label: Theorem
concepts.definition: A mathematical statement that has been proven true

concepts.label: Lemma
concepts.definition: A proven statement used as a stepping stone to prove a theorem

concepts.label: Axiom
concepts.definition: A statement accepted as true without proof

concepts.label: Conjecture
concepts.definition: A mathematical statement believed to be true but not yet proven

concepts.label: Infinity
concepts.definition: A concept describing something without bound or end

concepts.label: Countable
concepts.definition: A set that can be put in one-to-one correspondence with natural numbers

concepts.label: Uncountable
concepts.definition: A set that cannot be put in one-to-one correspondence with natural numbers

concepts.label: Topology
concepts.definition: The study of properties preserved under continuous deformation

concepts.label: Open Set
concepts.definition: A set where every point has a neighborhood contained in the set

concepts.label: Closed Set
concepts.definition: A set that contains all its limit points

concepts.label: Metric
concepts.definition: A function that defines distance between points in a space

concepts.label: Norm
concepts.definition: A function that assigns a length to vectors

concepts.label: Inner Product
concepts.definition: A generalization of dot product that defines angles and lengths

concepts.label: Eigenvalue
concepts.definition: A scalar that represents how much an eigenvector is scaled by a transformation

concepts.label: Eigenvector
concepts.definition: A vector that maintains its direction under a linear transformation

concepts.label: Linear Transformation
concepts.definition: A function that preserves vector addition and scalar multiplication

concepts.label: Basis
concepts.definition: A linearly independent set that spans a vector space

concepts.label: Dimension
concepts.definition: The number of vectors in a basis for a vector space

concepts.label: Span
concepts.definition: The set of all linear combinations of a collection of vectors

concepts.label: Linear Independence
concepts.definition: Vectors where no vector can be written as a combination of others

concepts.label: Rank
concepts.definition: The dimension of the column space of a matrix

concepts.label: Null Space
concepts.definition: The set of vectors that map to zero under a linear transformation

concepts.label: Inverse
concepts.definition: A function that undoes the operation of another function

concepts.label: Bijection
concepts.definition: A function that is both one-to-one and onto

concepts.label: Injection
concepts.definition: A function where different inputs give different outputs

concepts.label: Surjection
concepts.definition: A function where every element in the codomain is mapped to

concepts.label: Composition
concepts.definition: The operation of applying one function to the result of another

concepts.label: Isomorphism
concepts.definition: A bijective function that preserves mathematical structure

concepts.label: Homomorphism
concepts.definition: A function that preserves mathematical operations

concepts.label: Group
concepts.definition: A set with an operation satisfying closure, associativity, identity, and inverses

concepts.label: Ring
concepts.definition: A set with two operations (addition and multiplication) satisfying certain axioms

concepts.label: Field
concepts.definition: A ring where every non-zero element has a multiplicative inverse

concepts.label: Vector Space
concepts.definition: A set of vectors with operations of addition and scalar multiplication

concepts.label: Subspace
concepts.definition: A subset of a vector space that is closed under vector operations

concepts.label: Quotient
concepts.definition: The result of division

concepts.label: Remainder
concepts.definition: What's left after division when the divisor doesn't divide evenly

concepts.label: GCD
concepts.definition: Greatest Common Divisor, the largest number that divides two or more numbers

concepts.label: LCM
concepts.definition: Least Common Multiple, the smallest positive number divisible by two or more numbers

concepts.label: Modular Arithmetic
concepts.definition: Arithmetic where numbers wrap around after reaching a certain value

concepts.label: Congruence
concepts.definition: Two numbers having the same remainder when divided by a modulus
